gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Post-FW-update state persistence: No test verifies that the language selection UI correctly reflects the newly installed language pack immediately after a firmware update completes. Existing tests (66363556, 66363557, 66363558) cover the DFU trigger and update flow itself, but none validate that the settings screen refreshes to show the updated language without requiring an app restart.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Stale UI state after FW update: The bug shows the *previous* language still displayed after update. No existing test checks for stale cached language values in the UI layer after a firmware update completes. This is a UI refresh/sync gap between the device firmware state and the app's in-memory settings cache.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — 'Update currently not available' dialog on language change attempt: Test 66363583 covers this dialog persisting after earbud reconnection on Voyager Free 60+, but does NOT cover the scenario where the dialog appears after a *successful firmware update* when trying to change language again. The trigger condition is different — stale post-update state vs. disconnected device.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — App restart as workaround: No test validates that the language settings UI and language change functionality are restored to correct behavior only after an app kill/relaunch. This is a critical lifecycle gap — the bug requires app restart to resolve, and no existing test covers this recovery path.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — Missing multi-device coverage: The bug scenario does not specify a device model. Existing tests (66363558, 66363583) are specific to Voyager Free 60+. There is no coverage for this language-stale-after-FW-update bug on other Poly devices like Surround 85, Voyager Surround 80, or Free 60 (non-plus). The bug may be device-agnostic or device-specific — no test covers the general case.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Language change blocked vs. FW update completion: No test verifies the exact state machine transition: after a FW update that includes a language pack, the app should allow a subsequent language change (since the FW is now current). The bug shows the app incorrectly blocks the next language change. This is a missing validation of the 'FW update completed → language change allowed' state transition.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-001: Post-FW-update language UI refresh — immediate validation after update completes
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language. Note current selection (English).
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
DO NOT restart app. Immediately navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Observe the displayed language selection.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language selection shows the language that was active before the update (English). 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): Language selection still shows English (correct in this case, but the UI may be stale from cache). The key validation is that the UI reflects the device's actual language state without requiring app restart. If the FW update changed the language pack, the new language must be displayed. If no language change occurred, English must still be displayed — but it must be the LIVE device state, not a stale cached value.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-002: Stale UI cache detection — change language, FW update, verify UI shows stale previous language
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language. Set language to French.
Verify French is now displayed and active.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
DO NOT restart app. Immediately navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Observe the displayed language selection.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language selection shows French (the language set before the update). 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): Language selection incorrectly shows English (the pre-change default), indicating the app is displaying a stale cached value from before the language was changed, and the UI did not refresh from the device's actual firmware state after the update completed.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-003: 'Update currently not available' dialog after successful FW update — immediate language change attempt
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
DO NOT restart app. Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Attempt to change language from English to German.
Observe the dialog/behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language changes to German successfully — no dialog shown. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): App displays 'Update currently not available' dialog when attempting to change language, even though the FW update just completed successfully. This indicates the app's state machine incorrectly believes a firmware update is still in progress or the language change is blocked due to stale post-update state.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-004: App restart recovery — language change unblocked after kill/relaunch
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
Attempt to change language from English to German. (Expected to fail with 'Update currently not available' dialog per bug.)
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents).
Relaunch app. Wait for device to reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Attempt to change language from English to German.
Observe the behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language changes to German successfully — no dialog shown. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): 'Update currently not available' dialog still appears even after app restart, indicating the stale state is persisted beyond app lifecycle and requires device re-pairing or cache clear. The critical validation is that app restart IS the expected workaround and MUST resolve the issue.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-005: IMMEDIATE STATE TRIGGER — Change language, IMMEDIATELY kill app before navigating away, reopen to verify persistence
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Change language from English to Spanish.
IMMEDIATELY (within 1 second, do NOT navigate away or switch tabs) force-kill the app via swipe from recents.
Relaunch app. Wait for device to reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Observe the displayed language selection.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language selection shows Spanish. The language change was committed to the device firmware before the app was killed. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): Language selection shows English, indicating the app's in-memory state was not flushed/persisted to the device before the app was terminated, or the app relies on a local cache that was lost on kill. This validates immediate state saving/sync timing.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-006: DEEP LIFECYCLE — FW update, phone reboot, then verify language UI and change functionality
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
Reboot the phone (power off, then power on).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app. Wait for device to reconnect.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Observe the displayed language selection.
Attempt to change language from English to Italian.
Observe the behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language selection shows English (or the language set before the update). Language changes to Italian successfully — no dialog shown. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): Language selection shows a stale/wrong value, or the 'Update currently not available' dialog appears when attempting to change language. A phone reboot should fully clear any stale app cache and force a fresh read from the device firmware.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-009: STATE MACHINE — FW update completed → language change allowed (multiple sequential changes)
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Change language from English to French. Verify success.
Change language from French to German. Verify success.
Change language from German to Spanish. Verify success.
Change language from Spanish back to English. Verify success.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: All four language changes succeed without any dialog. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): The first language change after FW update may show 'Update currently not available' dialog (per bug), or subsequent changes may fail. This validates the full state machine transition: 'FW update completed → language change allowed' is not a one-shot state — it must remain unlocked for all subsequent changes.

generated_testcases.title: TC-LANG-FW-010: Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect after FW update — language UI and change functionality
steps: Precondition: Device connected, app open, current language = English.
Trigger firmware update: Navigate to Device → Software & Updates → Install available update.
Wait for update to complete (device restarts, reconnects automatically).
Disconnect Bluetooth (from phone settings or app).
Reconnect Bluetooth. Wait for app to recognize device.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Language.
Observe the displayed language selection.
Attempt to change language from English to French.
Observe the behavior.
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Language selection shows English. Language changes to French successfully — no dialog shown. 🧠 BUG SIGNATURE: Actual (If Bug): Language selection shows a stale value, or 'Update currently not available' dialog appears. A Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect should force the app to re-read device state, clearing any stale in-memory cache from the post-FW-update period.
